title: US-Iran ceasefire narrative vs. physical evidence — May 9 2026
narrative: Trump administration claims the Iran ceasefire is "still in effect" while striking Iranian tankers. Pentagon/CENTCOM statements say attacks are "defensive" and "proportionate." State Dept frames tanker strikes as enforcing blockade legally. Marco Rubio demands Iran give "immediate response." Shell CEO says 12% of global crude is off the market.
telemetry: USS Spruance disabled Iranian tanker Touska — fired into engine room, not a defensive patrol
Multiple vessels transiting Strait of Hormuz hit by Iranian gunfire; physical evidence strait is NOT passable
ADS-B/satellite tracking: zero vessels broke through US blockade since Monday, per CENTCOM own records
Maersk CEO: Iran war costing $500M/month in extra shipping costs; shares dropping sharply
Shell CEO on record: "hard reality of taking 12% of the world's crude off the market" — energy impact severe
13 US troops killed, 381 wounded in 40 days of Operation Epic Fury — absent from official ceasefire framing
Russia providing Iran cyber support and spy satellite imagery of US military facilities — per Ukraine intel
CNBC headline: 'US economy showing no signs of pain' — directly contradicted by Maersk, Shell, and oil market data
analysis:
The "ceasefire in effect" narrative collapses under the weight of telemetry. USS Spruance did not fire into an Iranian tanker's engine room as a "defensive patrol" — that is an offensive engagement against a civilian vessel in international waters. Vessels transiting the Strait of Hormuz are being hit by Iranian gunfire, confirmed by multiple independent reports; the strait is functionally impassable, contradicting any claim of a functioning blockade or a ceasefire.

CENTCOM's own satellite and ADS-B data reportedly shows zero vessels broke through the US blockade since Monday — meaning the blockade is a physical fact, not a diplomatic fiction. If the ceasefire were holding, Iranian tankers would be moving freely; they are not. The Shell CEO's explicit statement that 12% of global crude is off the market is not a diplomatic talking point — it is a hard physical consequence of a naval blockade that is actively being defended with force.

The casualty figures — 13 US troops killed, 381 wounded in 40 days — are incompatible with a ceasefire. 'Proportionate defensive operations' do not produce those numbers over six weeks. The administration is framing an active naval blockade with kinetic engagements as peace; the telemetry says otherwise.

Russia providing Iran spy satellite imagery of US military facilities adds a second layer: the ceasefire narrative may be useful to Iran and Russia as diplomatic cover while the physical war continues and intelligence cooperation deepens. The 'peace talks' framing gives both sides political room while the blockade and strikes continue.
verdict: SIPHONED VERDICT: The ceasefire is a diplomatic label on an active naval blockade; the physical evidence shows zero vessels breaching it, Iranian gunfires closing the strait, and 13 US troops dead — official "peace" framing is entirely untethered from operational reality.
